message:
Simplify guarded push config

Replace the managed two-remote setup with a single-origin workflow.
Guarded branches now switch to SSH via includeIf.onbranch while
normal fetches and topic pushes stay on HTTPS.

Keep the pre-push hook as a backstop, add a status helper, and
cover the new behavior with round-trip tests.

files.patch:
@@ -142,6 +142,7 @@ if status is-interactive
   abbr -g gclg '__git_clone_guarded'
   abbr -g gguard 'git-guard-pushes'
   abbr -g gunguard 'git-unguard-pushes'
+  abbr -g gguardst 'git-guarded-push-status'
   abbr -g gcf 'git config --list'
   abbr -g gclean 'git clean -fd'
   abbr -g gcp 'git cherry-pick'

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+function git-guarded-push-status --description "Show guarded push status for a repo"
+    ~/.config/git/guarded-pushes-status $argv
+end

@@ -0,0 +1,126 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+repo='.'
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+Usage: guarded-pushes-status [repo]
+
+Show the effective guarded-push configuration for a repository.
+EOF
+}
+
+die() {
+  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
+  exit 1
+}
+
+absolute_path_from_repo() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local path="$2"
+
+  case "$path" in
+    /*)
+      printf '%s\n' "$path"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      printf '%s\n' "$repo_root/$path"
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+git_common_dir() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local path
+
+  path="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-common-dir)"
+  absolute_path_from_repo "$repo_root" "$path"
+}
+
+resolve_include_path() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local include_path="$2"
+
+  if [ -z "$include_path" ]; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  case "$include_path" in
+    /*)
+      printf '%s\n' "$include_path"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      printf '%s/%s\n' "$(git_common_dir "$repo_root")" "$include_path"
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    -h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    --)
+      shift
+      break
+      ;;
+    -*)
+      die "unknown option: $1"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      if [ "$repo" != '.' ]; then
+        die "only one repository path may be provided"
+      fi
+      repo="$1"
+      shift
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+[ $# -eq 0 ] || die "only one repository path may be provided"
+
+repo_root="$(git -C "$repo" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
+[ -n "$repo_root" ] || die "$repo is not a Git repository"
+
+remote_name="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemote 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [ -z "$remote_name" ]; then
+  remote_name='origin'
+fi
+
+guarded_branches="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushManagedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [ -z "$guarded_branches" ]; then
+  guarded_branches="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
+fi
+
+fetch_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url "$remote_name" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+effective_push_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url --push "$remote_name" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+base_push_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "remote.$remote_name.pushurl" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+include_path="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushIncludePath 2>/dev/null || true)"
+resolved_include_path="$(resolve_include_path "$repo_root" "$include_path")"
+current_branch="$(git -C "$repo_root" symbolic-ref --quiet --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)"
+
+push_mode='unknown'
+case "$effective_push_url" in
+  git@*:*|ssh://git@*/*)
+    push_mode='SSH'
+    ;;
+  http://*|https://*)
+    push_mode='HTTPS'
+    ;;
+esac
+
+printf 'Guarded push config for %s\n' "$repo_root"
+printf '  remote: %s\n' "$remote_name"
+printf '  fetch url: %s\n' "${fetch_url:-<missing>}"
+printf '  base push url: %s\n' "${base_push_url:-<none>}"
+printf '  effective push url: %s\n' "${effective_push_url:-<missing>}"
+printf '  guarded branches: %s\n' "${guarded_branches:-<none>}"
+printf '  include path: %s\n' "${resolved_include_path:-<none>}"
+if [ -n "$current_branch" ]; then
+  printf '  current branch: %s\n' "$current_branch"
+else
+  printf '  current branch: <detached>\n'
+fi
+printf '  current push mode: %s\n' "$push_mode"

@@ -44,6 +44,7 @@ is_protected_branch() {
   local pattern
 
   for pattern in $protected_branches; do
+    # shellcheck disable=SC2254
     case "$branch" in
       $pattern)
         return 0
@@ -60,7 +61,7 @@ allowed_remote() {
 }
 
 blocked_branches=""
-while read -r local_ref local_sha remote_ref remote_sha; do
+while read -r _local_ref _local_sha remote_ref _remote_sha; do
   case "$remote_ref" in
     refs/heads/*)
       branch="${remote_ref#refs/heads/}"
@@ -100,15 +101,17 @@ printf '  branch(es): %s\n' "$blocked_branches" >&2
 printf '  remote:     %s\n' "${remote_name:-<unknown>}" >&2
 printf '  url:        %s\n' "${remote_url:-<unknown>}" >&2
 printf '\n' >&2
-printf 'This guard keeps guarded branches off HTTPS remotes so they still\n' >&2
-printf 'require your SSH agent (for example Secretive/Touch ID). Topic branch\n' >&2
-printf 'pushes are unaffected.\n' >&2
+printf 'This guard keeps guarded branches on SSH so pushes still require your\n' >&2
+printf 'SSH agent (for example Secretive/Touch ID). Topic branch pushes are\n' >&2
+printf 'still allowed over HTTPS.\n' >&2
+printf '\n' >&2
+printf 'If this repository is configured with guarded pushes, try:\n' >&2
+printf '  git checkout %s && git push\n' "${blocked_branches%% *}" >&2
 
 if ssh_url="$(to_ssh_url "$remote_url" 2>/dev/null)"; then
-  printf '\nSuggested setup:\n' >&2
-  printf '  git remote add %s-ssh %s\n' "${remote_name:-origin}" "$ssh_url" >&2
+  printf '\nOr push explicitly via SSH:\n' >&2
   for branch in $blocked_branches; do
-    printf '  git config branch.%s.pushRemote %s-ssh\n' "$branch" "${remote_name:-origin}" >&2
+    printf '  git push %s HEAD:%s\n' "$ssh_url" "$branch" >&2
   done
 fi

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-script_dir="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)"
+script_dir="$(CDPATH='' cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)"
 shared_hook="$script_dir/hooks/pre-push-guarded-branches"
 marker='installed-by=setup-guarded-pushes'
 legacy_marker='installed-by=setup-protected-pushes'
@@ -21,13 +21,13 @@ branches push via SSH.
 Options:
   --branch <name>      Guard this branch (repeatable, default: main)
   --remote <name>      Primary remote to use for fetch/topic pushes (default: origin)
-  --ssh-remote <name>  SSH remote for guarded pushes (default: <remote>-ssh)
+  --ssh-remote <name>  Legacy option; accepted for migration and ignored
   -h, --help           Show this help
 
 Examples:
   setup-guarded-pushes
   setup-guarded-pushes ~/code/tenzir/ship
-  setup-guarded-pushes --branch main --branch release ~/code/tenzir/ship
+  setup-guarded-pushes --branch main --branch 'release/*' ~/code/tenzir/ship
 EOF
 }
 
@@ -82,27 +82,52 @@ convert_remote_urls() {
   esac
 }
 
-absolute_git_dir() {
+absolute_path_from_repo() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local git_dir
+  local path="$2"
 
-  git_dir="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-dir)"
-  case "$git_dir" in
+  case "$path" in
     /*)
-      printf '%s\n' "$git_dir"
+      printf '%s\n' "$path"
       ;;
     *)
-      printf '%s\n' "$repo_root/$git_dir"
+      printf '%s\n' "$repo_root/$path"
       ;;
   esac
 }
 
+git_common_dir() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local path
+
+  path="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-common-dir)"
+  absolute_path_from_repo "$repo_root" "$path"
+}
+
+git_path() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local relative_path="$2"
+  local path
+
+  path="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-path "$relative_path")"
+  absolute_path_from_repo "$repo_root" "$path"
+}
+
+guarded_include_name() {
+  printf 'guarded-pushes.conf\n'
+}
+
+guarded_include_path() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+
+  printf '%s/%s\n' "$(git_common_dir "$repo_root")" "$(guarded_include_name)"
+}
+
 install_hook() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local git_dir hook_path hook_dir local_hook
+  local hook_path hook_dir local_hook
 
-  git_dir="$(absolute_git_dir "$repo_root")"
-  hook_path="$git_dir/hooks/pre-push"
+  hook_path="$(git_path "$repo_root" hooks/pre-push)"
   hook_dir="$(dirname "$hook_path")"
   local_hook="$hook_dir/pre-push.local"
 
@@ -143,52 +168,147 @@ branches_config() {
   printf '%s\n' "$joined"
 }
 
-sync_managed_branch_push_remotes() {
+get_recorded_branch_push_remote() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local push_remote="$2"
-  local primary_remote="$3"
-  local previous_managed branch
+  local branch="$2"
+  local recorded_branch recorded_remote
+
+  while IFS=$'\t' read -r recorded_branch recorded_remote; do
+    if [ "$recorded_branch" = "$branch" ]; then
+      printf '%s\n' "$recorded_remote"
+      return 0
+    fi
+  done < <(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get-all hooks.protectedPushOriginalBranchPushRemote 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+  return 1
+}
+
+record_original_branch_push_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local original_remote
+
+  if get_recorded_branch_push_remote "$repo_root" "$branch" >/dev/null; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  original_remote="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [ -n "$original_remote" ]; then
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add hooks.protectedPushOriginalBranchPushRemote \
+      "$branch"$'\t'"$original_remote"
+  else
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add hooks.protectedPushOriginalBranchPushRemote \
+      "$branch"$'\t'"__unset__"
+  fi
+}
+
+record_original_branch_push_remotes() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch
+
+  for branch in "${branches[@]}"; do
+    record_original_branch_push_remote "$repo_root" "$branch"
+  done
+}
+
+restore_branch_push_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local original_remote
+
+  if original_remote="$(get_recorded_branch_push_remote "$repo_root" "$branch")"; then
+    if [ "$original_remote" = '__unset__' ]; then
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true
+    else
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.pushRemote" "$original_remote"
+    fi
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true
+}
+
+previous_managed_branches() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local previous_managed
 
   previous_managed="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushManagedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
   if [ -z "$previous_managed" ]; then
     previous_managed="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
   fi
 
+  printf '%s\n' "$previous_managed"
+}
+
+sync_managed_branch_push_remotes() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local primary_remote="$2"
+  local legacy_ssh_remote="$3"
+  local previous_managed branch tracking_remote
+
+  previous_managed="$(previous_managed_branches "$repo_root")"
+
   for branch in $previous_managed; do
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true
-    if git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.remote" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    restore_branch_push_remote "$repo_root" "$branch"
+    tracking_remote="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.remote" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [ -n "$legacy_ssh_remote" ] && [ "$tracking_remote" = "$legacy_ssh_remote" ]; then
       git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.remote" "$primary_remote"
     fi
   done
 
   for branch in "${branches[@]}"; do
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.pushRemote" "$push_remote"
-    if git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.remote" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-      git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.remote" "$push_remote"
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.pushRemote" "$primary_remote"
+    tracking_remote="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.remote" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [ -n "$legacy_ssh_remote" ] && [ "$tracking_remote" = "$legacy_ssh_remote" ]; then
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.remote" "$primary_remote"
     fi
   done
 
   git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushManagedBranches "$(branches_config)"
 }
 
-restrict_ssh_remote_fetch() {
+sync_branch_includes() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local ssh_remote="$2"
-  local branch
+  local include_path="$2"
+  local previous_managed branch
+
+  previous_managed="$(previous_managed_branches "$repo_root")"
+
+  for branch in $previous_managed; do
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "includeIf.onbranch:$branch.path" 2>/dev/null || true
+  done
 
-  git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "remote.$ssh_remote.fetch" 2>/dev/null || true
   for branch in "${branches[@]}"; do
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add "remote.$ssh_remote.fetch" \
-      "+refs/heads/$branch:refs/remotes/$ssh_remote/$branch"
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add "includeIf.onbranch:$branch.path" "$include_path"
   done
+
+  git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushIncludePath "$include_path"
+}
+
+write_guarded_push_include() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local remote_name="$2"
+  local ssh_url="$3"
+  local include_path config_path
+
+  include_path="$(guarded_include_path "$repo_root")"
+  config_path="$(guarded_include_name)"
+  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$include_path")"
+
+  cat > "$include_path" <<EOF
+# $marker
+[remote "$remote_name"]
+	pushurl = $ssh_url
+EOF
+
+  printf '%s\n' "$config_path"
 }
 
 record_original_state_if_unmanaged() {
   local repo_root="$1"
   local remote_name="$2"
-  local ssh_remote_name="$3"
-  local current_remote_url="$4"
-  local git_dir hook_path original_push_default original_ssh_remote_url original_ssh_fetch
+  local current_remote_url="$3"
+  local hook_path original_push_default original_push_url
 
   if git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemote >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     return 0
@@ -199,34 +319,25 @@ record_original_state_if_unmanaged() {
     return 0
   fi
 
-  git_dir="$(absolute_git_dir "$repo_root")"
-  hook_path="$git_dir/hooks/pre-push"
+  hook_path="$(git_path "$repo_root" hooks/pre-push)"
   if hook_is_managed "$hook_path"; then
     return 0
   fi
 
   original_push_default="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get remote.pushDefault 2>/dev/null || true)"
-  original_ssh_remote_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url "$ssh_remote_name" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  original_push_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "remote.$remote_name.pushurl" 2>/dev/null || true)"
 
   git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemote "$remote_name"
-  git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushSshRemote "$ssh_remote_name"
   git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemoteOriginalUrl "$current_remote_url"
   if [ -n "$original_push_default" ]; then
     git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalPushDefault "$original_push_default"
   else
     git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalPushDefault '__unset__'
   fi
-  if [ -n "$original_ssh_remote_url" ]; then
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshRemoteUrl "$original_ssh_remote_url"
-  else
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshRemoteUrl '__missing__'
-  fi
-  original_ssh_fetch="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get-all "remote.$ssh_remote_name.fetch" 2>/dev/null \
-    | tr '\n' '|' | sed 's/|$//' || true)"
-  if [ -n "$original_ssh_fetch" ]; then
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshFetch "$original_ssh_fetch"
+  if [ -n "$original_push_url" ]; then
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalPushUrl "$original_push_url"
   else
-    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshFetch '__none__'
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalPushUrl '__unset__'
   fi
 }
 
@@ -285,32 +396,21 @@ repo_root="$(git -C "$repo" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
 origin_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url "$remote_name" 2>/dev/null || true)"
 [ -n "$origin_url" ] || die "remote '$remote_name' does not exist in $repo_root"
 
-record_original_state_if_unmanaged "$repo_root" "$remote_name" "$ssh_remote_name" "$origin_url"
+record_original_state_if_unmanaged "$repo_root" "$remote_name" "$origin_url"
+record_original_branch_push_remotes "$repo_root"
 convert_remote_urls "$origin_url" || die "cannot derive HTTPS/SSH URLs from: $origin_url"
+include_path="$(write_guarded_push_include "$repo_root" "$remote_name" "$ssh_url")"
 
-current_ssh_url="$(git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url "$ssh_remote_name" 2>/dev/null || true)"
-if [ -n "$current_ssh_url" ] && [ "$current_ssh_url" != "$ssh_url" ]; then
-  printf 'warning: updating %s from %s to %s\n' "$ssh_remote_name" "$current_ssh_url" "$ssh_url" >&2

+sync_branch_includes "$repo_root" "$include_path"
+sync_managed_branch_push_remotes "$repo_root" "$remote_name" "$ssh_remote_name"
 
 git -C "$repo_root" remote set-url "$remote_name" "$https_url"
-if git -C "$repo_root" remote get-url "$ssh_remote_name" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-  git -C "$repo_root" remote set-url "$ssh_remote_name" "$ssh_url"
-else
-  git -C "$repo_root" remote add "$ssh_remote_name" "$ssh_url"
-fi
-
-# Restrict origin-ssh to only fetch guarded branches so topic branches on
-# origin and origin-ssh don't create checkout ambiguity.
-restrict_ssh_remote_fetch "$repo_root" "$ssh_remote_name"
-
-git -C "$repo_root" config --local remote.pushDefault "$remote_name"
+git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "remote.$remote_name.pushurl" 2>/dev/null || true
 git -C "$repo_root" config --local hooks.protectedBranches "$(branches_config)"
-sync_managed_branch_push_remotes "$repo_root" "$ssh_remote_name" "$remote_name"
 
 install_hook "$repo_root"
 
 printf 'Configured guarded pushes for %s\n' "$repo_root"
-printf '  %s -> %s\n' "$remote_name" "$https_url"
-printf '  %s -> %s\n' "$ssh_remote_name" "$ssh_url"
+printf '  %s fetch/topic pushes -> %s\n' "$remote_name" "$https_url"
+printf '  %s guarded pushes -> %s\n' "$remote_name" "$ssh_url"
 printf '  guarded branches: %s\n' "$(branches_config)"

@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@ Remove the guarded-push setup from a repository.
 
 Options:
   --remote <name>      Primary remote name (default: stored value or origin)
-  --ssh-remote <name>  SSH remote name (default: stored value or <remote>-ssh)
+  --ssh-remote <name>  Legacy SSH remote name (default: stored value or <remote>-ssh)
   -h, --help           Show this help
 
 Examples:
@@ -36,28 +36,68 @@ hook_is_managed() {
   grep -Fq "$marker" "$hook_path" || grep -Fq "$legacy_marker" "$hook_path"
 }
 
-absolute_git_dir() {
+config_file_is_managed() {
+  local path="$1"
+
+  [ -e "$path" ] || return 1
+  grep -Fq "$marker" "$path" || grep -Fq "$legacy_marker" "$path"
+}
+
+absolute_path_from_repo() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local git_dir

+
+  case "$path" in

+
+  path="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-path "$relative_path")"
+  absolute_path_from_repo "$repo_root" "$path"
+}
+
+git_common_dir() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local path

+  absolute_path_from_repo "$repo_root" "$path"

+resolve_include_path() {

-  git_dir="$(git -C "$repo_root" rev-parse --git-dir)"
-  case "$git_dir" in
+  case "$include_path" in

-      printf '%s\n' "$git_dir"
+      printf '%s\n' "$include_path"

-      printf '%s\n' "$repo_root/$git_dir"
+      printf '%s/%s\n' "$(git_common_dir "$repo_root")" "$include_path"

 restore_hook() {
   local repo_root="$1"
-  local git_dir hook_path local_hook
+  local hook_path local_hook
 
-  git_dir="$(absolute_git_dir "$repo_root")"
-  hook_path="$git_dir/hooks/pre-push"
-  local_hook="$git_dir/hooks/pre-push.local"
+  hook_path="$(git_path "$repo_root" hooks/pre-push)"
+  local_hook="$(dirname "$hook_path")/pre-push.local"
 
   if ! hook_is_managed "$hook_path"; then
     return 0
@@ -77,6 +117,144 @@ unset_if_set() {
   git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "$key" 2>/dev/null || true
 }
 
+previous_managed_branches() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local previous_managed
+
+  previous_managed="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushManagedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [ -z "$previous_managed" ]; then
+    previous_managed="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get hooks.protectedBranches 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  fi
+
+  printf '%s\n' "$previous_managed"
+}
+
+get_recorded_branch_push_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local recorded_branch recorded_remote
+
+  while IFS=$'\t' read -r recorded_branch recorded_remote; do
+    if [ "$recorded_branch" = "$branch" ]; then
+      printf '%s\n' "$recorded_remote"
+      return 0
+    fi
+  done < <(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get-all hooks.protectedPushOriginalBranchPushRemote 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+  return 1
+}
+
+restore_branch_push_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local original_remote current_remote
+
+  if original_remote="$(get_recorded_branch_push_remote "$repo_root" "$branch")"; then
+    if [ "$original_remote" = '__unset__' ]; then
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true
+    else
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.pushRemote" "$original_remote"
+    fi
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  current_remote="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  case "$current_remote" in
+    "$remote_name"|"$ssh_remote_name")
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.pushRemote" 2>/dev/null || true
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+get_recorded_branch_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local recorded_branch recorded_remote
+
+  while IFS=$'\t' read -r recorded_branch recorded_remote; do
+    if [ "$recorded_branch" = "$branch" ]; then
+      printf '%s\n' "$recorded_remote"
+      return 0
+    fi
+  done < <(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get-all hooks.protectedPushOriginalBranchRemote 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+  return 1
+}
+
+restore_branch_remote() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local branch="$2"
+  local fallback_remote="$3"
+  local original_remote current_remote
+
+  if original_remote="$(get_recorded_branch_remote "$repo_root" "$branch")"; then
+    if [ "$original_remote" = '__unset__' ]; then
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "branch.$branch.remote" 2>/dev/null || true
+    else
+      git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.remote" "$original_remote"
+    fi
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  current_remote="$(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get "branch.$branch.remote" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [ -n "$fallback_remote" ] && [ "$current_remote" = "$ssh_remote_name" ]; then
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local "branch.$branch.remote" "$fallback_remote"
+  fi
+}
+
+restore_ssh_remote_fetch() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local ssh_remote_name="$2"
+  local recorded=false
+  local fetch_spec old_fetch_spec
+
+  git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "remote.$ssh_remote_name.fetch" 2>/dev/null || true
+
+  while IFS= read -r fetch_spec; do
+    recorded=true
+    case "$fetch_spec" in
+      __none__)
+        ;;
+      *'|'*)
+        IFS='|' read -ra _fetch_specs <<< "$fetch_spec"
+        for old_fetch_spec in "${_fetch_specs[@]}"; do
+          [ -n "$old_fetch_spec" ] && git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add \
+            "remote.$ssh_remote_name.fetch" "$old_fetch_spec"
+        done
+        ;;
+      *)
+        git -C "$repo_root" config --local --add "remote.$ssh_remote_name.fetch" "$fetch_spec"
+        ;;
+    esac
+  done < <(git -C "$repo_root" config --local --get-all hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshFetch 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+  if [ "$recorded" = false ]; then
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local "remote.$ssh_remote_name.fetch" \
+      "+refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/$ssh_remote_name/*"
+  fi
+}
+
+remove_branch_includes() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local previous_managed branch
+
+  previous_managed="$(previous_managed_branches "$repo_root")"
+  for branch in $previous_managed; do
+    git -C "$repo_root" config --local --unset-all "includeIf.onbranch:$branch.path" 2>/dev/null || true
+  done
+}
+
+remove_include_file() {
+  local repo_root="$1"
+  local include_path="$2"
+  local resolved_path
+
+  resolved_path="$(resolve_include_path "$repo_root" "$include_path")"
+  if [ -n "$resolved_path" ] && config_file_is_managed "$resolved_path"; then
+    rm -f "$resolved_path"
+  fi
+}
+

@@ -0,0 +1,201 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+script_dir="$(CDPATH='' cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)"
+repo_root="$(CDPATH='' cd -- "$script_dir/.." && pwd)"
+setup_script="$repo_root/config/setup-guarded-pushes"
+unguard_script="$repo_root/config/unguard-pushes"
+status_script="$repo_root/config/guarded-pushes-status"
+
+tmpdirs=()
+last_repo=''
+
+cleanup() {
+  local dir
+
+  if [ "${#tmpdirs[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  for dir in "${tmpdirs[@]}"; do
+    if [ -n "$dir" ]; then
+      rm -rf "$dir"
+    fi
+  done
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+fail() {
+  printf 'FAIL: %s\n' "$*" >&2
+  exit 1
+}
+
+assert_eq() {
+  local expected="$1"
+  local actual="$2"
+  local message="$3"
+
+  if [ "$expected" != "$actual" ]; then
+    printf 'FAIL: %s\nexpected: %s\nactual:   %s\n' "$message" "$expected" "$actual" >&2
+    exit 1
+  fi
+}
+
+assert_unset() {
+  local repo="$1"
+  local key="$2"
+  local message="$3"
+
+  if git -C "$repo" config --local --get-all "$key" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    fail "$message"
+  fi
+}
+
+assert_not_exists() {
+  local path="$1"
+  local message="$2"
+
+  if [ -e "$path" ]; then
+    fail "$message"
+  fi
+}
+
+git_common_dir() {
+  local repo="$1"
+  local path
+
+  path="$(git -C "$repo" rev-parse --git-common-dir)"
+  case "$path" in
+    /*)
+      printf '%s\n' "$path"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      printf '%s\n' "$repo/$path"
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+make_repo() {
+  local tmpdir repo remote_url
+
+  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
+  tmpdirs+=("$tmpdir")
+  repo="$tmpdir/repo"
+  mkdir -p "$repo"
+
+  git -C "$repo" init -q -b main
+  : > "$repo/README.md"
+  git -C "$repo" add README.md
+  git -C "$repo" -c user.name=Test -c user.email=test@example.com commit -qm init
+  remote_url='git@github.com:example/project.git'
+  git -C "$repo" remote add origin "$remote_url"
+
+  last_repo="$repo"
+}
+
+test_single_remote_pushurl_behavior() {
+  local repo include_path status_output
+
+  make_repo
+  repo="$last_repo"
+
+  "$setup_script" "$repo" >/dev/null
+
+  assert_eq https://github.com/example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url origin)" \
+    'setup should normalize origin to HTTPS for fetch/topic pushes'
+  assert_eq origin "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get branch.main.pushRemote)" \
+    'guarded branches should push to origin by default'
+  assert_eq git@github.com:example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url --push origin)" \
+    'main should push via SSH through the guarded include'
+
+  git -C "$repo" checkout -q -b topic/demo
+  assert_eq https://github.com/example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url --push origin)" \
+    'topic branches should still push via HTTPS'
+
+  git -C "$repo" checkout -q main
+  include_path="$(git_common_dir "$repo")/$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushIncludePath)"
+  if [ ! -f "$include_path" ]; then
+    fail 'setup should create the guarded include file'
+  fi
+
+  status_output="$("$status_script" "$repo")"
+  case "$status_output" in
+    *'current push mode: SSH'*)
+      ;;
+    *)
+      fail 'status helper should report SSH mode on guarded branches'
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+test_round_trip_restores_original_remote_state() {
+  local repo include_path
+
+  make_repo
+  repo="$last_repo"
+  git -C "$repo" config --local branch.main.pushRemote upstream
+  git -C "$repo" config --local remote.origin.pushurl ssh://git@github.com/example/project.git
+
+  "$setup_script" "$repo" >/dev/null
+  assert_eq origin "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get branch.main.pushRemote)" \
+    'setup should replace the guarded branch pushRemote while active'
+
+  include_path="$(git_common_dir "$repo")/$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get hooks.protectedPushIncludePath)"
+  "$unguard_script" "$repo" >/dev/null
+
+  assert_eq git@github.com:example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url origin)" \
+    'unguard should restore the original origin URL'
+  assert_eq ssh://git@github.com/example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get remote.origin.pushurl)" \
+    'unguard should restore the original local pushurl'
+  assert_eq upstream "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get branch.main.pushRemote)" \
+    'unguard should restore the original branch pushRemote'
+  assert_not_exists "$include_path" 'unguard should remove the managed include file'
+  assert_unset "$repo" hooks.protectedPushIncludePath 'unguard should remove include metadata'
+}
+
+test_legacy_multi_remote_migrates_cleanly() {
+  local repo
+
+  make_repo
+  repo="$last_repo"
+  git -C "$repo" remote set-url origin https://github.com/example/project.git
+  git -C "$repo" remote add origin-ssh git@github.com:example/project.git
+  git -C "$repo" config --local branch.main.remote origin-ssh
+  git -C "$repo" config --local branch.main.pushRemote origin-ssh
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedBranches main
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushManagedBranches main
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemote origin
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushSshRemote origin-ssh
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushPrimaryRemoteOriginalUrl git@github.com:example/project.git
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalPushDefault __unset__
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshRemoteUrl __missing__
+  git -C "$repo" config --local hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshFetch __none__
+
+  "$unguard_script" "$repo" >/dev/null
+  "$setup_script" "$repo" >/dev/null
+
+  assert_eq https://github.com/example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url origin)" \
+    'migration should keep origin on HTTPS after re-setup'
+  assert_eq origin "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get branch.main.remote)" \
+    'migration should restore guarded branches to tracking origin'
+  assert_eq origin "$(git -C "$repo" config --local --get branch.main.pushRemote)" \
+    'migration should collapse guarded pushes back to origin'
+  assert_eq git@github.com:example/project.git \
+    "$(git -C "$repo" remote get-url --push origin)" \
+    'migration should make guarded pushes use SSH through origin'
+  if git -C "$repo" remote get-url origin-ssh >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    fail 'migration should remove the legacy origin-ssh remote when it was tool-managed'
+  fi
+}
+
+test_single_remote_pushurl_behavior
+test_round_trip_restores_original_remote_state
+test_legacy_multi_remote_migrates_cleanly
+
+printf 'ok: guarded push tests passed\n'
